commonMedications.name: Semaglutide / GLP-1 (Ozempic) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: EOPYY covers for diabetes with endocrinologist prescription

medicine.pharmacyAccess: Φαρμακεία (pharmacies) with green cross signage in all Greek cities and towns. Rotating 24h duty pharmacy system. EU prescriptions accepted.
medicine.notes: Greece EOPYY provides pharmaceutical coverage with 0-25% copay depending on medication category and patient status. Pensioners and chronic condition patients often pay 0-10%. Private pharmacies may charge slightly more than reference prices. EU EHIC card accepted.

plans.name: Google Fi Flexible (US Number)
plans.notes: Line 1 $20 + Line 2 $15 = $35 base. Data $10/GB × 3GB = $30. Works in Greece at same rate. Keeps US number for banking/2FA.

plans.name: Cosmote Postpaid
plans.notes: 2 lines ~€10/line. Cosmote is largest carrier. EU roaming included.

categories.name: Dining Out
categories.seniorDiscounts: Taverna menus affordable. No tipping expected but 5-10% appreciated.
categories.notes: Lunch for 2 at taverna ~€20-22, 2x/week = ~€180/mo = ~$200/mo in Rhodes, Greece.

categories.name: NFL RedZone
categories.notes: DAZN NFL Game Pass €19.99/mo during season. Annualized ~€13/mo (~$15).

categories.name: Gym/Fitness
categories.seniorDiscounts: Municipal gyms (dimotiko gymnastirio) offer senior rates.

categories.name: Parks/Outdoors
categories.seniorDiscounts: EU citizens 65+ free entry to archaeological sites and museums.
categories.notes: Free beaches, parks, hiking trails, ancient sites (seniors free).

categories.name: Books/Media
categories.notes: Public libraries limited but free. Budget for books.

categories.name: Coffee/Cafes
categories.seniorDiscounts: No specific discounts. Kafeneio culture affordable.

monthlyPass.notes: Rhodes transit pass. Seniors 65+ eligible for reduced fares (up to 50% off) on OASA transit in Athens. Island transit discounts vary.

publicTransit.coverage: Athens metro, tram, and bus network. KTEL intercity buses. Island locations rely on ferries and local buses.
publicTransit.seniorDiscount: Seniors 65+ eligible for reduced fares (up to 50% off) on OASA transit in Athens. Island transit discounts vary.

rideShare.availability: Uber available in Athens. BEAT app popular. Taxis with meters throughout. Island taxis may use fixed fares.

registration.notes: Road tax (teli) annual, based on engine size. KTEO inspection every 2 years.

insurance.notes: Third-party mandatory, from ~$20/mo. Comprehensive $30-50/mo. Rates among lowest in EU.

fuel.notes: Unleaded ~$1.65-1.75/L. Diesel ~$1.55-1.65/L. Island fuel prices may be 5-10% higher.

parking.notes: Controlled parking zones in cities. Free parking easier on islands and smaller towns.

tolls.notes: Estimated monthly toll/highway costs for Rhodes area.

carOwnership.seniorDiscounts: Seniors 65+ eligible for reduced fares (up to 50% off) on OASA transit in Athens. Island transit discounts vary.

transportation.walkability: Excellent walkability in town centers and islands. Compact Greek towns designed for pedestrians.

carFree.notes: Transit pass + occasional ride-share. Limited transit — may need more ride-share trips.

withCar.notes: Includes insurance, fuel, parking, maintenance. Moderate suburban costs.

eyeExam.notes: Exam ~20 EUR. EOPYY (public health) covers ophthalmologist referral. Private ~20-30 EUR.

glasses.notes: Basic frames+lenses ~50 EUR. Progressive ~120 EUR. EOPYY provides partial glasses reimbursement with referral.

insurance.coverage: EOPYY covers ophthalmology + partial glasses reimbursement with prescription
insurance.notes: No standalone vision insurance. Private health (Interamerican, Ethniki) may include optical. EOPYY reimburses ~40-100 EUR for glasses with prescription.

cleaning.notes: Katharismos ~30 EUR. Very limited public dental via EOPYY. Most dental is private.

filling.notes: Sfragisma ~40 EUR. Private dental very affordable by EU standards.

crown.notes: Stefani porselanis ~250 EUR. Greece is a dental tourism destination. Quality excellent.

insurance.coverage: Private dental plan, free cleanings, 30% off treatments
insurance.notes: Dental plans ~10 EUR/mo through private insurers. EOPYY covers minimal preventive dental only.

items.name: Water
items.notes: Tap safe in most areas; mineral preferred

items.name: Premium dog food
items.notes: Royal Canin/Hills from pet shops or vet clinics

breakdown.notes: Renter's insurance not standard in most European countries. Utilities billed separately.

housing.taxNotes: No rental tax for tenants. ENFIA (property tax) is landlord responsibility. Stamp duty 3.6% on annual rent (typically landlord pays).
housing.leaseTerms: 3-year minimum lease (even if contract says less). Security deposit 2 months rent. Landlord must give 3-month notice; tenant 1-month notice.
housing.marketNotes: Dodecanese island. Moderate costs. Mix of old town and modern rentals. Seasonal tourist impact.

stores.name: Sklavenitis
stores.notes: Largest Greek chain after Marinopoulos merger. Good prices.

stores.name: AB Vassilopoulos
stores.notes: Ahold Delhaize owned. Premium selection, good fresh departments.

stores.name: Lidl
stores.notes: Best prices on staples. Expanding rapidly in Greece.

stores.name: Masoutis
stores.notes: Strong in northern Greece. Good regional products.

stores.name: Laïkí agorá
stores.notes: Weekly street markets (λαϊκή αγορά). Best prices on produce, olives, cheese, fish.

healthcare.system: EOPYY (National Organization for Healthcare) + Private supplement
healthcare.coverageSummary: Greece provides public healthcare via EOPYY for residents with social insurance. Retirees can access via bilateral agreements or voluntary EOPYY enrollment. Private insurance affordable and common for expats.

costs.notes: Comprehensive policy, age-rated. AMD specialist coverage.
€20-40/visit private. EOPYY copay €5 for contracted doctors.
Private for AMD. Public wait can be weeks.
Quarterly blood tests. €10-20 private or covered by EOPYY.

blackHairCare.label: Hair Care
blackHairCare.notes: Very limited Afro hair salons. Athens has a few in Omonoia/Victoria area. Bring own products.

providers.name: Afro hair salons (Athens)
providers.notes: Limited options, mainly in immigrant neighborhoods

stores.name: Εκπτώσεις (seasonal sales)
stores.notes: Sales in Jan/Feb and Jul/Aug — 30-60% off

discounts.name: OASA Senior Transit Card
discounts.description: Reduced-rate public transport (50% off) for 65+ in Athens/Thessaloniki.

discounts.name: OSE Senior Rail Discount
discounts.description: 25% off rail travel for 60+ with OSE/Hellenic Train.

discounts.name: Free museum entry
discounts.description: EU citizens 65+ get free entry to all state archaeological sites and museums.

discounts.name: EOPYY medication copay
discounts.description: Pensioners pay 10% copay on chronic disease medications (vs 25% standard). Some medications free.

discounts.name: KAPI Senior Centers
discounts.description: Municipal senior centers (ΚΑΠΗ) offering free meals, healthcare screening, social activities for 60+.
